item.title: Silent Ransom Group Hits US Law Firms in Escalating Extortion Attacks
item.description:
The financially motivated group is combining vishing, IT impersonation, and in-person office intrusions to steal data and extort victims.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: AI Slop Will Kill Cybersecurity Storytelling If We Let It
item.description:
AI-generated content threatens credibility in cybersecurity. This "Ask the Expert" column explores why human oversight matters and how to maintain authentic narratives.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Russian Attackers Weaponize WinRAR Flaw Against Ukrainian Orgs
item.description:
Two separate campaigns target CVE-2025-8088, fixed last July, to conduct data theft and cyberespionage against military and government targets in Ukraine.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Microsoft Exchange Flaw Lets Attackers Spoof Any Email Address
item.description:
“Ghost-Sender" is the result of a widespread misconfiguration, according to researchers, and evidence indicates it's being actively abused in the wild.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: The Invisible Battlefield: How Cyberwar Is Reshaping Everyday Life
item.description:
Former National Cyber Director Chris Inglis warns that cyberattacks threaten hospitals, utilities, and essential services.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Nightmare-Eclipse Drops Yet Another Microsoft Exploit, RoguePlanet
item.description:
The disgruntled researcher released yet another PoC for a Windows Defender bug that allows for system takeover, showing no signs of abandoning their ongoing feud with Microsoft.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Bug Bounty Research Triggers ServiceNow Security Alert
item.description:
Security research inadvertently led organizations to believe they were being breached through their ServiceNow instances.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: CISA Rewrites Federal Patching Requirements for AI Threat Era
item.description:
The new directive gives federal agencies three days to fix the most dangerous flaws, while less severe issues can be deferred.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Chinese, N. Korean Threat Groups Build on Asia-Pacific Success
item.description:
North Korea's gross domestic product (GDP) has grown, in part because of the nation's state-sponsored cybercrime groups, which target financial firms and other businesses.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Max-Severity Ivanti Flaw Exploited 24 Hours After Disclosure
item.description:
Initial methods suggest attackers had likely mapped out Ivanti's asset landscape upfront and acted quickly once the exploit became public.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Phishing Attack Volume Down 20%, But Risk Still Rising
item.description:
Hackers are valuing quality over quantity, using AI to upgrade their phishing attacks rather than multiply them.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Claude Fable 5 Doesn't Change the Mythos Security Story
item.description:
Stay cool: Mythos 5 is an upgrade over Mythos Preview while Fable 5 is Mythos "made safe for general use," Anthropic explained.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Alert Fatigue Is Becoming a Security Threat of Its Own
item.description:
As alert volumes outpace human capacity, organizations are turning to AI, automation, and deeper context to separate real threats from the noise. The post Alert Fatigue Is Becoming a Security Threat of Its Own appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Oracle Addresses PeopleSoft Vulnerability Amid Reports of Zero-Day Attacks
item.description:
Oracle has released mitigations for CVE-2026-35273, but it has not said whether it’s a zero-day exploited in ShinyHunters attacks. The post Oracle Addresses PeopleSoft Vulnerability Amid Reports of Zero-Day Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Google Confirms Exploitation of Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day by ShinyHunters
item.description:
Oracle has mitigated CVE-2026-35273, but it has not publicly confirmed the vulnerability’s in-the-wild exploitation. The post Google Confirms Exploitation of Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day by ShinyHunters appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Ivanti Sentry Exploitation Attempts Hitting Honeypots
item.description:
The critical-severity OS command injection vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary code with root privileges. The post Ivanti Sentry Exploitation Attempts Hitting Honeypots appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Iranian Cyber Group Handala Claims Cal Water Hack
item.description:
The hackers published 5GB of data, including customer personal information and credentials for the RTKBase platform. The post Iranian Cyber Group Handala Claims Cal Water Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: In Other News: Google Security Layoffs, AudiA6 Takedown, $400 Million Coupang Fine
item.description:
Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: ICS device exposure remains flat as attack surface widens, Microsoft issues incident response playbook for AI, IBM and AT&T accused of hack cover-ups. The post In Other News: Google Security Layoffs, AudiA6 Takedown, $400 Million Coupang Fine appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
item.description:
A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
item.description:
An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
item.description:
Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
item.description:
Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
item.description:
Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequ
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
item.description:
The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
item.description:
A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Coupang hit with record $409 million data breach fine in Korea
item.description:
​​The Personal Information Protection Commission (PIPC), South Korea's data protection regulator, has fined e-commerce giant Coupang a record 624.6 billion won (roughly $409 million) following a massive data breach affecting more than 37 million customers [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Authorities dismantle 'AudiA6' ransomware crypto-laundering service
item.description:
Law enforcement has dismantled the “AudiA6” cryptocurrency service allegedly used by ransomware actors and other cybercriminals to launder more than $380 million. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Oracle mitigates PeopleSoft zero-day exploited in data theft attacks
item.description:
Oracle is warning about a critical PeopleSoft Suite zero-day vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-35273 that allows unauthenticated remote code execution, with the flaw actively exploited in ShinyHunter data theft attacks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Maine breach portal abused to publish fake data breach disclosures
item.description:
In an unusual misinformation campaign, fraudulent data breach disclosures were submitted to Maine's official breach portal and publicly posted before their legitimacy could be verified, prompting companies to deny the claims. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Over 73,000 French govt employees affected in Tchap messenger breach
item.description:
The French government revealed that a recent breach of its Tchap encrypted messaging platform affects the accounts of over 73,000 employees in the French public sector. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Pharma giant Novo Nordisk discloses breach of clinical trials data
item.description:
Danish pharmaceutical giant Novo Nordisk, the world's largest producer of insulin, disclosed a data breach affecting patient information from some clinical trials. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Early Warning Signs of Supply-Chain Attacks Live in the Dark Web
item.description:
GitHub access sales, leaked repositories, and stolen API keys can all become supply-chain attack footholds. Flare explores how underground forums expose early signals tied to software supply-chain risk. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Over 400 Arch Linux packages compromised to push rootkit, infostealer
item.description:
More than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) are distributing a Linux rootkit and infostealer malware targeting credentials and access tokens. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Ukrainian national pleads guilty to role in Conti ransomware operation
item.description:
A Ukrainian national extradited from Ireland to the United States last year has pleaded guilty to conspiracy charges tied to the Conti ransomware operation. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Maine disables data breach notification portal after fake disclosures
item.description:
Maine has taken its public data breach reporting portal offline after fraudulent breach disclosures were published on the state's website, prompting a review of procedures to prevent abuse in the future. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Unknown
item.description:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: 67 million Thais exposed in massive data leak, parliament launches probe
item.description:
Pattaya Mail reports: A civil society group has petitioned a parliamentary committee to investigate a massive data breach after a government agency leaked the national ID numbers and healthcare details of approximately 67.1 million people. Thanarat Kuawattanaphan, a software expert leading the group, submitted the petition to Alongkot Maneekat, chairman of the House Committee on... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Saskatoon man facing extradition for cyberattacks on American institutions
item.description:
Shane Fraser reports: A Saskatoon man who allegedly conspired to install malware, steal login credentials, and mine cryptocurrency from American educational institutions is facing extradition to the United States. The cyberattack accusations were levelled against Ryan James Roach in Saskatoon Court of King’s Bench, where he was ordered to be taken into custody to await extradition following... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: WA: Chelan County enters third week of disruptions with no recovery timeline
item.description:
On June 8, Andrew Simpson reported: Chelan County entered its third week of system-wide disruptions Monday following a malware incident discovered over Memorial Day weekend, with officials saying they still do not have a timeline for restoring affected systems. According to a June 8 update, county officials became aware of malware affecting the county network... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Suspected Russian Hacker Arrested and Charged in the United States
item.description:
Nate Raymond reports: A suspected Russian hacker is now in U.S. custody following his arrest in Thailand last year and has been charged with facilitating a campaign of cyberattacks carried out by a Russia-aligned group that victimized numerous U.S. companies. Denis Obrezko, 36, made his initial appearance in federal court in Boston on Tuesday in... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Women’s health advocacy organization prepares mass suit against Clinical Diagnostics
item.description:
In July 2025, the Nova ransomware gang stole cervical cancer screening data on 850,000 women held by Clinical Diagnostics (“Eurofins”). The lab paid them an undisclosed ransom amount, but that has not been the end of the lab’s problems. In May 2026, the Dutch Health and Youth Care Inspectorate concluded that the laboratory had failed... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Former Saydel schools IT worker sentenced for Iowa cyber sabotage
item.description:
Today’s reminder of the insider threat is brought to us by DysruptionHub: A former Saydel Community School District information technology worker in Iowa was sentenced June 11 after prosecutors said he disrupted school technology systems used by students and staff. The disruptions affected classroom technology, staff accounts and district-managed devices after Ezekiel Dean Potter left... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: After a Massive Hack, Global Schools Group’s Negotiator Acted “Bizarrely.” It Didn’t End Well for Them.
item.description:
The bigger they are, the harder they fail? Global Schools Foundation (GSF) is a Singapore-headquartered, not-for-profit K–12 education organization. With a global network of schools, the foundation strives to provide world-class education to students across multiple countries. Global Schools Group (GSG), an initiative of GSF, manages and operates a network of 12 international school brands... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: ShinyHunters Targets Education Sector with Oracle PeopleSoft Exploit
item.description:
From Mandiant and Google Threat Intelligence Group, an advisory: Mandiant and Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) have identified an active compromise and extortion campaign attributed to UNC6240 (ShinyHunters) targeting Oracle PeopleSoft application infrastructure. The activity was observed between May 27, 2026, and June 9, 2026 and is consistent with the exploitation of CVE-2026-35273, a critical remote... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Labcorp reaches $35M settlement over American Medical Collection Agency breach
item.description:
Do you remember the horrific American Medical Collection Agency (Retrieval-Masters Creditors Bureau Inc.) breach in 2019? You can refresh your memory by scrolling through the Related posts below this one, but TL;DR: LabCorp was one of AMCA’s clients that was affected by the breach, and in July 2019, they notified HHS that 10,251,784 patients had... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Ukrainian national pleads guilty to role in Conti ransomware operation
item.description:
Lawrence Abrams reports: A Ukrainian national extradited from Ireland to the United States last year has pleaded guilty to conspiracy charges tied to the Conti ransomware operation. The U.S. Department of Justice announced Thursday that 44-year-old Oleksii Oleksiyovych Lytvynenko pleaded guilty to conspiracy to commit wire fraud for his role in Conti ransomware attacks conducted... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Southern Illinois Ob-Gyn Associates Announces Data Breach Affecting 38,700 Individuals
item.description:
A data breach at Southern Illinois Ob-Gyn Associates has affected 38,700 individuals. Data breaches have also been reported by Wellpoint […] The post Southern Illinois Ob-Gyn Associates Announces Data Breach Affecting 38,700 Individuals appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Senator Seeks Answers from NYC Health & Hospitals About 1.8M Record Breach
item.description:
The Senate Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions (HELP) Committee Chair Senator Bill Cassidy, M.D. (R-LA), is seeking answers from NYC […] The post Senator Seeks Answers from NYC Health & Hospitals About 1.8M Record Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Data Breaches Announced by Two Digestive Health Companies
item.description:
Cyberattacks and data breaches have recently been announced by the national gastroenterology medical group Gastro Health and Spokane Digestive Disease […] The post Data Breaches Announced by Two Digestive Health Companies appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Florida Law Firm Data Breach Affects 65,000 Individuals
item.description:
A cyberattack at the law firm GrayRobinson has affected 65,000 individuals. Data breaches have also been announced by C2N Diagnostics […] The post Florida Law Firm Data Breach Affects 65,000 Individuals appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Labcorp Agrees to $35M Settlement to Resolve AMCA Data Breach Litigation
item.description:
A $35,000,000 settlement has been agreed to resolve a long-running class action lawsuit against Labcorp over a 2018 cybersecurity incident […] The post Labcorp Agrees to $35M Settlement to Resolve AMCA Data Breach Litigation appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach
